name: Ishmael
creator: Herman Melville
practice: Participant observation, cetology, the ethnography of work, classification as meaning-making
approach: To understand the whale, Ishmael studies everything — the anatomy, the industry, the mythology, the workers. His cetology chapters are thick description of a workplace. He classifies whales not by species but by folio size — because how you categorize determines what you see.
body_of_work: Ships out on the Pequod to understand whaling. Documents every role, every practice, every belief. His classification system (whale as folio, as quarto, as duodecimo) is a commentary on how taxonomy shapes perception. The whiteness of the whale IS the chapter on meaning-making.
why_instructive: He shows that the dome's data categories SHAPE what the dome can see. How you classify determines what you find. His obsessive documentation is what the data layer aspires to — but his warning is clear: the thing itself always exceeds the classification.

dome_relevance.6: Economic — the whaling industry as complete economic system
dome_relevance.8: Community — the ship as community with its own culture
dome_relevance.11: Creativity — classification itself as creative act, meaning-making through documentation

sphere_relevance.3: Environmental — the ocean as environment, the whale as ecosystem
sphere_relevance.5: Social — the ship's social hierarchy and multi-racial community
sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — whaling's history, mythology, and decline
